headline: Test-time compute surges to a dominant position this week as a cluster of inference-side techniques breaks out together.
body: Test-time compute is the standout mover of the week, posting a momentum of 145 and a ratio of 145, the highest readings across all tracked techniques. Reinforcing that rotation, several structurally related techniques enter as new outliers simultaneously: task decomposition, prompt chaining, multi-model workflow, and tool calling all break into the rankings for the first time, pointing to a broad shift in attention toward runtime reasoning architectures rather than training-time approaches. On the weakening side, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, workflow orchestration, and agent wrappers all pull back sharply, consistent with the inference-side reorientation. Meanwhile, code generation breaks out as a new entrant alongside a strong week for AI-assisted coding, and NVIDIA's Cosmos 3 and Nemotron 3 Ultra releases [5] add fresh context around inference acceleration [0] and GPU utilization, while GitHub Copilot's move to usage-based pricing [6] and Colorado rolling back its AI governance law [7] round out a structurally busy week.

cited_items.title: Mellum2 Goes Open Source: A Fast Model for AI Workflows
